acceptance_targets: [ch13_dialogue_01] In the revised exchange around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:137 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:149, a cold reader must be able to identify Lola and Earl by syntax and premise alone: Lola's speech should loop, self-correct, or braid pressure through story-adjacent language, while Earl's should be one hard, spare verdict grounded in collective self-protection. If Earl's line can still be swapped with Lola's objection without changing the scene's logic, fail.

[ch13_dialogue_02] The revised beat at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:259 must sound like Harry and add concrete pressure. A reader should hear organizer-accountant logic, not mentor-figure wisdom; if the line still works as a generic moral lesson that any older character could deliver, fail.

[ch13_prose_01] Before the legal-calculation beat at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:171 or its replacement, the revised stretch must contain at least two separate Carmen-internal moments that are unmistakably thought, sensation, memory, or self-assessment. If the sequence can still be read straight through as external blocking and dialogue with no direct Carmen interiority for 10 or more paragraphs, fail.

[ch13_craft_01] In the revised span beginning after snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:197, there may be only one explicit dialogue beat whose function is "Marcus will ignore the vote." Every later beat must add a new fact, action, or relationship shift visible on the page. If two consecutive retained beats can still both be summarized as "they all know he's going to do it anyway," fail.
[length_chapter_13_min_words] Chapter word count is >= 4000 while preserving heading contract.

findings.acceptance_test: In the revised exchange around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:137 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:149, a cold reader must be able to identify Lola and Earl by syntax and premise alone: Lola's speech should loop, self-correct, or braid pressure through story-adjacent language, while Earl's should be one hard, spare verdict grounded in collective self-protection. If Earl's line can still be swapped with Lola's objection without changing the scene's logic, fail.
findings.problem: The chapter's advertised five-way fracture partially collapses here. Lola's objection is delivered as a clean summary of her day/night split, and Earl's supposed verdict mostly paraphrases her "we knew who we were helping" logic. Two different characters end up doing the same rhetorical work in the center of the confrontation, which weakens both idiolect separation and the chapter's promised moral split.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework Lola's objection at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:137 so it sounds like Lola under stress: faster, braided, self-correcting, and tied to Tammy/daytime-nighttime pressure without turning into a neat thesis statement. Rebuild Earl's intervention at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:149 so it lands as his own terse collective-trust or solidarity verdict rather than a restatement of Lola's point. Use the reaction beats at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:139 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:151 to show the room registering two genuinely different objections.

findings.acceptance_test: The revised beat at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:259 must sound like Harry and add concrete pressure. A reader should hear organizer-accountant logic, not mentor-figure wisdom; if the line still works as a generic moral lesson that any older character could deliver, fail.
findings.problem: Harry's closing admonition, "If you report, make sure it's because you need to. Not because you're angry," reads like polished motive-summary rather than Harry's established ledger-like register. It turns him into a tidy moral diagnostician and gives the aftermath an authorial paraphrase after the chapter has already landed its pressure.
findings.rewrite_direction: Either cut the closing admonition at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:259 or replace it with a line grounded in Harry's own idiom at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:239 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:259: exposure, counts, timing, leverage, or what reporting will actually set in motion on paper. If Harry addresses motive at all, make it arrive through operational language rather than abstract emotional coaching.

findings.acceptance_test: Before the legal-calculation beat at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:171 or its replacement, the revised stretch must contain at least two separate Carmen-internal moments that are unmistakably thought, sensation, memory, or self-assessment. If the sequence can still be read straight through as external blocking and dialogue with no direct Carmen interiority for 10 or more paragraphs, fail.
findings.problem: The late confrontation drops out of Carmen's interior for thirteen consecutive paragraphs before returning to her legal calculation. In a close-third Carmen chapter, that run starts reading like blocked stage dialogue rather than lived pressure moving through her body and mind.
findings.rewrite_direction: Thread brief interior beats back into the stretch spanning snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:145 through snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:169. Use Carmen's body, perception, or thought at one or more of snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:147, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:151, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:157, or snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:161 so the Earl-Harry-Marcus turn remains filtered through her rather than through neutral camera coverage.

findings.acceptance_test: In the revised span beginning after snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:197, there may be only one explicit dialogue beat whose function is "Marcus will ignore the vote." Every later beat must add a new fact, action, or relationship shift visible on the page. If two consecutive retained beats can still both be summarized as "they all know he's going to do it anyway," fail.
findings.problem: Once Marcus's false note lands, the aftermath keeps restating the same conclusion in cleaner language: he agreed too fast, Agnes says he'll do it anyway, Carmen says he'll do it anyway, Harry says probably, then Carmen worries through a clean count. The scene keeps translating suspicion instead of converting it into a sharper post-vote consequence or countermeasure.
findings.rewrite_direction: Compress and re-sequence the aftermath from snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:217 through snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:275. Preserve the necessary beats: Agnes's refusal to sort, Harry's concession about risk distribution, Carmen's clean count, and Carmen's dread. But make each retained beat add new dramatic work. After the false note at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:197, at least one later beat should change tactics, expose a new operational vulnerability, or alter the Carmen-Harry alliance instead of merely restating that Marcus may lie.

findings.acceptance_test: Chapter word count is >= 4000 while preserving heading contract.
findings.rewrite_direction: Add causally necessary scene material that strengthens the chapter engine, increases pressure, and lands the planned state shift without filler.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This is a dark comedy about elderly agency, institutional failure, and moral compromise in a setting most fiction treats as a punchline or a tragedy but never both. The risks the novel must take: render the comedy of octogenarians as pharmaceutical distributors without condescending to them or turning them into lovable rogues; make Carmen's escalating complicity fully legible without moralizing about it; show the bodily realities of aging — incontinence, tremor, cognitive decline, falls, the smell of a body that doesn't leave a building — with unsentimental specificity; render the economic desperation of both healthcare workers and uninsured buyers without turning either into social-issue abstractions; give the moral ambiguity of the ring genuine weight by refusing to resolve it into either 'they were wrong' or 'they were heroes.' The novel must trust readers to sit with complexity. When in doubt between too raw and too safe, choose raw. A nursing home that smells like potpourri is a nursing home written by someone who's never been in one.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism for bodily functions, aging, economic shame, institutional dysfunction, or the realities of nursing home care is a craft failure. The novel's comedy and humanity depend on rendering what polite fiction glosses over: what it smells like to clean someone who can't clean themselves, what it costs to count coins for a bus fare, what oxycodone sells for per milligram on the secondary market, what a concussion looks like on an 88-year-old skull, what it sounds like when someone with Parkinson's tries to hold a coffee cup steady, what Burt's dementia actually looks like up close. If the prose flinches from these specifics, the novel loses its authority.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the default for all dialogue. Uncontracted forms appear only for emphasis, character-specific formality (Agnes's low contraction level), Carmen under extreme stress reverting to formal speech, or deliberate register choices. In a contemporary New Jersey nursing home, stiff diction is the anomaly, not the norm.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate high-intensity scenes with reflective interiority. Vary scene count: single-scene chapters for maximum compression, three-scene chapters for range. Comic chapters (3, 9) should feel structurally different from quiet character studies (7, 8, 12, 15), which should feel different from crisis chapters (11, 16, 17). Follow a confrontation with a reflection, an escalation with a domestic scene, a comic set piece with an elegiac one. If two consecutive chapters share the same rhythm, one needs redesigning.
prose_style_profile.diction: Grounded and specific. Drug names (oxycodone, alprazolam, hydroxychloroquine), nursing procedures (med pass, pill count, charting, fall assessment), body realities (incontinence, tremor, laceration, scalp bleed). No purple prose, no literary performance. The style should feel like someone who works with their hands is telling you the truth about what she saw.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, varying by POV character. Carmen chapters are warm and sensory, anchored in the body and the immediate environment. Harry chapters are precise and calculating, with attention to numbers and angles. Agnes chapters are clinical and compressed. Lola chapters are winding and emotionally layered, with embedded memories. Earl chapters are spare and weighted, with silence doing as much work as language. Marcus chapters are kinetic, with physical movement and code-switching. Tammy chapters are flat and observational, her trained eye cataloguing what doesn't add up.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Varied by chapter and POV. Carmen chapters favor medium sentences with occasional long runs when she's anxious. Harry chapters are short and clipped. Comic set pieces (chapters 3 and 9) move between fast inventory and slow domestic beats. Quiet chapters (7, 12, 15) slow down and let space onto the page. No chapter should feel rhythmically identical to the one before it.
